stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with her own beliefs, even when that conflicts with the position of her party.
substantive: She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, she regularly emphasises the benefits of her political positions for people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with her own beliefs, even when that conflicts with the position of her party. She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, she regularly emphasises the benefits of her political positions for people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
